This course exposes students to the application of psychological principles to problems in law. There is an emphasis on the American trial system, correction systems, and civil commitment. Prerequisites: PSY 2100 and junior/senior standing or permission of the instructor.

This course explores how we develop a sense of self through physical, cognitive, and socioemotional development and experiences. We’ll discuss how developmental experiences, particularly attachment with caregivers and peers, can support and hinder the development of self as well as contemporary theories for secure attachment and relating through repair of developmental interruptions. Prerequisites: PSY 2100 and junior/senior standing or permission of the instructor.
